summary: This is a fictional narrative chapter following John and his friends Brian and Dave at a bagel shop the morning after a night of smoking weed. The story reveals backstory about Dave's brother's suicide and its devastating effect on his family, while John realizes that cops who visited him were actually looking for his father, not him. The chapter establishes the dynamics between three high school friends - Brian the crass but loyal one, Dave the traumatized stoner, and John the narrator with an absent, possibly troubled father. The tone blends mundane teenage life with darker undercurrents of family tragedy and mystery.
key_points: Dave's older brother committed suicide when they were freshmen, leading Dave to cope through heavy marijuana use while his mother became a recluse and his sister fled to California
Brian is characterized as a Stifler-type who uses his mom's credit card (from a settlement) while making friends Venmo him - simultaneously generous and scamming
John realizes the cops were looking for his father Jonathan, not him, connecting to his dad's previous workplace where Dave's brother also worked
The father is described as a 'Kaczynski freak' who 'bugged out,' suggesting he may have done something radical or criminal
The chapter hints at something sinister about the workplace that employed both John's father and Dave's deceased brother
money_quotes: He called himself a gentleman, but not in the creepy Elliot Rodger way, or in anything resembling the real meaning of the word.
His mom's credit card and we Venmo him. That's the type of guy he was.
Like we would smoke and get high, but for Dave it was a different thing. He would smoke till he was catatonic.
He graduated valedictorian and she never did. Maybe that wasn't enough?
Dave would go to dark places and Brian couldn't be brought there. So it worked.
Last night I thought my world was closing in, now I realize this just all isn't my problem.
At least my dad isn't some kind of Kaczynski freak.
key_insight: This fictional chapter explores how teenage friendships form around shared brokenness - absent fathers, family tragedy, and coping mechanisms - while hinting at a larger mystery connecting the characters' families.
